definition: existing as an idea rather than a concrete or physical thing
example: The lecture dealt with abstract concepts that were hard to visualize.

definition: to gradually gather or increase in amount over time
example: Sediment continued to accumulate at the bottom of the lake.

definition: sufficient or good enough for a particular purpose
example: The scholarship provided adequate funding to cover her tuition.

definition: to publicly support or recommend a particular cause or policy
example: Many scientists advocate stronger measures to protect the environment.

definition: open to more than one interpretation; not clear
example: The instructions were so ambiguous that students interpreted them differently.

definition: to examine something in detail to understand or explain it
example: Researchers analyzed the data to identify meaningful patterns.

definition: to expect or predict that something will happen
example: The company anticipated a rise in demand during the holidays.

definition: clearly visible or easily understood; seeming to be true
example: It soon became apparent that the theory had serious flaws.

definition: based on random choice rather than reason or system
example: The committee's decision seemed arbitrary and lacked clear criteria.

definition: to evaluate or judge the nature or quality of something
example: Teachers assess student progress through regular examinations.

definition: to regard something as being caused by a particular thing
example: She attributed her success to years of consistent effort.

definition: logical and consistent, forming a unified whole
example: A good essay presents a coherent argument from start to finish.

definition: to fall down or break apart suddenly and completely
example: The old bridge collapsed under the weight of the flood.

definition: to make up for something or provide something in return
example: The bonus was meant to compensate employees for extra hours.

definition: to gather information and arrange it in a systematic way
example: The team compiled the survey results into a detailed report.

definition: including all or nearly all elements or aspects
example: The textbook offers a comprehensive overview of world history.

definition: to form an idea or plan in the mind
example: The architect conceived a bold design for the new museum.

definition: as a result of a preceding action or situation
example: He forgot his notes and consequently struggled during the presentation.

definition: notably large in size, amount, or degree
example: The project required a considerable investment of time and money.

definition: to be or make up the parts of something
example: Water constitutes about seventy percent of the human body.

definition: to assert the opposite of a statement; to be inconsistent with
example: The new evidence seemed to contradict the earlier findings.

definition: causing strong disagreement or public debate
example: The policy proved highly controversial among local residents.

definition: based on what is generally accepted or traditionally done
example: She rejected conventional methods in favor of a creative approach.

definition: to have a mutual relationship in which one thing affects another
example: Studies show that sleep and academic performance strongly correlate.

definition: extremely important or decisive for a particular outcome
example: Timing is crucial when conducting this chemical experiment.

definition: increasing gradually by successive additions
example: The cumulative effect of small savings can be substantial over years.

definition: to reach a conclusion by reasoning from available evidence
example: From the clues, the detective deduced who had entered the room.

definition: lacking a necessary quality or element; insufficient
example: A diet deficient in vitamins can lead to serious health problems.

definition: to clearly show or prove something through evidence or action
example: The experiment demonstrated how plants respond to light.

definition: to use up or reduce the supply of something
example: Overfishing has depleted the region's once-abundant fish stocks.

definition: to obtain or develop something from a source
example: Many English words derive from Latin and Greek roots.

definition: to become progressively worse in quality or condition
example: Without maintenance, the roads gradually deteriorated.

definition: to make or become smaller, weaker, or less important
example: The medicine helped diminish the patient's pain.

definition: clearly different or separate from others of the same kind
example: Each species has distinct features that aid its survival.

definition: to twist or change something so it is misleading or inaccurate
example: Media coverage can distort the public's view of an event.

definition: showing a great deal of variety; very different
example: The university attracts students from diverse cultural backgrounds.

definition: most important, powerful, or influential
example: English has become the dominant language of scientific research.

definition: sudden and striking; involving a great change
example: There was a dramatic improvement in air quality after the ban.

definition: involving many carefully arranged parts; highly detailed
example: The ceremony followed an elaborate set of traditional rituals.

definition: to completely remove or get rid of something
example: The new process eliminated most of the waste from production.

definition: to come into view or become known
example: New evidence emerged that challenged the original theory.

definition: to give special importance or attention to something
example: The coach emphasized teamwork over individual talent.

definition: to meet or experience something, often unexpectedly
example: Travelers may encounter unfamiliar customs abroad.

definition: to improve the quality, value, or extent of something
example: Regular practice can greatly enhance your speaking skills.

definition: extremely large in size, amount, or degree
example: Building the dam required an enormous amount of concrete.

definition: to make certain that something will happen or be the case
example: Careful planning helps ensure the project stays on schedule.

definition: absolutely necessary; extremely important
example: Clean water is essential for human survival.

definition: plain or obvious; clearly seen or understood
example: Her talent for music was evident from an early age.

definition: to develop gradually over time
example: Languages evolve as new words enter everyday use.

definition: to go beyond a limit or greater than a set amount
example: The demand for tickets exceeded all expectations.

definition: to show or display a quality, feeling, or object publicly
example: The children exhibited great enthusiasm for the project.

definition: stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for doubt
example: The manual gave explicit instructions for assembling the device.

definition: to make full use of a resource or opportunity
example: The company exploited new technology to cut costs.

definition: to make an action or process easier
example: The new software facilitates communication between departments.

definition: possible to do easily or conveniently; practical
example: The engineers confirmed that the plan was technically feasible.

definition: to rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
example: Temperatures fluctuate widely between day and night in the desert.

definition: forming a necessary base or core; of central importance
example: Supply and demand is a fundamental principle of economics.

definition: to produce or create something
example: Wind turbines generate electricity without burning fuel.

definition: a system in which people or things are ranked by level or status
example: The company has a strict hierarchy of management positions.

definition: a proposed explanation made as a starting point for investigation
example: The scientists tested their hypothesis through repeated experiments.

definition: to explain or make clear by using examples or images
example: The teacher used a diagram to illustrate the water cycle.

definition: to force something such as a rule or burden onto others
example: The government imposed new taxes on imported goods.

definition: something that encourages or motivates a person to act
example: Tax breaks serve as an incentive for companies to invest.

definition: certain to happen and impossible to avoid
example: Some conflict is inevitable when people work closely together.

definition: to conclude something from evidence rather than explicit statements
example: From his tone, I inferred that he was disappointed.

definition: featuring new methods, ideas, or products
example: The startup won an award for its innovative design.

definition: to combine parts into a unified whole
example: The course integrates theory with practical fieldwork.

definition: of extreme force, strength, or degree
example: The athletes underwent an intense training program.

definition: to explain the meaning of something or understand it in a way
example: Historians interpret old documents to understand the past.

definition: very complicated or detailed
example: The clock contained an intricate arrangement of tiny gears.

definition: to show or prove that something is reasonable or right
example: The manager could not justify the sudden rise in prices.

definition: to handle or control something skillfully, sometimes unfairly
example: Scientists manipulate variables to observe their effects.

definition: to reduce something to the smallest possible amount or degree
example: Wearing a seatbelt minimizes the risk of injury.

definition: to make partial changes to something
example: Engineers modified the design to improve fuel efficiency.

definition: a belief, idea, or concept about something
example: The notion that the earth was flat persisted for centuries.

definition: not clearly expressed or easily understood; little known
example: The poem's meaning remained obscure even after careful reading.

definition: to get or acquire something, often through effort
example: You must obtain permission before entering the laboratory.

definition: to happen or take place
example: Earthquakes occur frequently along this fault line.

definition: an observable fact or event, especially a remarkable one
example: The northern lights are a stunning natural phenomenon.

definition: exact, accurate, and clearly defined
example: The recipe requires precise measurements of each ingredient.

definition: present as the strongest or main element
example: Agriculture was the predominant activity in the region.

definition: widespread or common in a particular area or time
example: Smartphones are now prevalent among teenagers everywhere.

definition: to formally forbid something by law or rule
example: The park rules prohibit lighting fires near the trees.

definition: important, well-known, or easily noticed
example: She is a prominent researcher in the field of genetics.

definition: to cause or encourage someone to do something
example: The warning prompted residents to evacuate immediately.

definition: to strengthen or support something
example: Repetition helps reinforce new vocabulary in memory.

definition: unwilling and hesitant to do something
example: He was reluctant to share his notes with classmates.

definition: to be similar to or look like something else
example: The young bird already resembles its parents in color.

definition: to limit the size, amount, or range of something
example: The new law restricts the sale of certain chemicals.

definition: to continue to have or keep something
example: Plants retain water in their leaves during dry seasons.

definition: to make something known or show what was hidden
example: The study revealed a surprising link between the two factors.

definition: extremely thorough, careful, or demanding
example: The medicine underwent rigorous testing before approval.

definition: large or important enough to have a noticeable effect
example: The reforms brought about a significant drop in pollution.

definition: coming after something in time; following
example: The discovery influenced all subsequent research in the field.

definition: large in amount, size, or importance
example: The charity received a substantial donation from the company.

definition: enough for a particular purpose
example: We had sufficient supplies to last the entire expedition.

definition: to weaken or damage something gradually
example: Constant criticism can undermine a person's confidence.

definition: the same in all cases and at all times; unchanging
example: The cells grew at a uniform rate under controlled conditions.

definition: to make practical and effective use of something
example: Farmers utilize modern equipment to increase their yields.

definition: absolutely necessary or essential
example: Bees play a vital role in pollinating crops.
